goal: Every agent turn writes a structured trace record to the database (model, provider, tool calls with names/args/result shape, latency per call, token counts); the chat UI surfaces a collapsible 'what ran' panel per response.
why: Operators currently fly blind -- they cannot see which model answered, which tools ran, or how long a turn took without tailing server logs. Phase 2 also depends on traces to confirm which mail path fires before one is removed.
success_criteria: After any agent response, the chat shows a disclosure row (e.g. '3 tools · Gemini Flash · 1.2 s') that expands to a per-tool trace with name, argument summary, and latency -- no network tab or log-tailing required.
public.agent_traces has one row per turn with model, provider, tool_calls (JSONB array), latency_ms, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens populated and non-null for every completed turn.
A slow or failed tool call is represented in the trace with its actual latency and an error field; the trace record is never silently omitted, even when the loop errors mid-turn.
The observability layer adds zero new runtime dependencies to the client bundle -- all writes happen server-side inside the existing SSE route.

tasks.title: agent_traces migration + trace persistence library
acceptance_criteria: Migration 0011_agent_traces.sql creates public.agent_traces with id, principal (check Wency/Jeanette), model, provider (check openrouter/gemini), tool_calls jsonb, latency_ms, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, error, created_at.
Migration enables RLS and declares NO policies (service-role lockdown matching 0010_pending_actions.sql).
Migration adds an index on (principal, created_at).
traces.ts exports insertTrace(input) returning the new row id, and getTrace(id) mapping snake_case to camelCase AgentTrace.
ToolCallTrace is { name, argsSummary, resultSummary, latencyMs, error }.
tasks.action: Write the migration in the comment+structure style of 0010_pending_actions.sql (header comment, create table if not exists, comment on table, index, enable row level security, no policies). In traces.ts import supabaseAdmin from @/lib/supabase/server; insertTrace inserts via .from('agent_traces').insert().select('id').single() and returns id; getTrace selects by id and maps to camelCase AgentTrace. Apply locally via npx supabase migration up -- never hand-apply to remote.

tasks.title: Instrument the agent loop + persist trace + expose over SSE and a fetch route
acceptance_criteria: Every completed turn writes exactly one agent_traces row with non-null model, provider, tool_calls (one entry per tool with name/argsSummary/resultSummary/latencyMs/error), latency_ms, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens.
A slow or failed tool call appears in tool_calls with measured latencyMs and a non-null error string on failure.
If the loop throws mid-turn, a trace row is still written with error set and partial tool calls -- never silently omitted.
The final SSE stream is unchanged plus one trailing data: {aquavoy_trace_id:<uuid>} line before data: [DONE]; the existing client parser ignores it.
GET /api/traces/<uuid> returns { ok:true, data: AgentTrace }; unknown id 404; missing principal 401 via getPrincipal.
tasks.action: In streamChatWithTools capture model+providerName from chatProvider(), time the turn, wrap executeTool for per-tool latencyMs/argsSummary/resultSummary/error, accumulate usage tokens (stream_options.include_usage). Wrap the final stream to pass chunks through, insertTrace before [DONE], emit data:{aquavoy_trace_id}. try/catch writes a trace on throw then re-throws. Add principal to ChatOptions; route passes getPrincipal. New GET /api/traces/[id]: getPrincipal gate, getTrace, 404 or {ok,data}.

tasks.title: Chat disclosure row + expandable per-tool trace panel
acceptance_criteria: After an assistant reply, a disclosure row renders under the bubble showing tool count, friendly provider/model label, and total latency in seconds (e.g. '3 tools · Gemini Flash · 1.2 s').
Clicking the row (a button with aria-expanded) toggles a panel listing each tool call's name, argsSummary, resultSummary, and per-tool latency; error tool calls render with --danger.
Row is keyboard-operable and screen-reader labeled; panel uses region semantics consistent with the existing history panel.
Metadata renders in --font-mono; surfaces use surface-step tokens; no hardcoded hex, no #000/#fff.
If the trace fetch fails or no trace id arrives, the bubble renders as today (row absent).
tasks.action: Extend Msg with trace?: AgentTrace. In send() SSE parsing capture aquavoy_trace_id, then fetch /api/traces/<id> and set the assistant message's trace (silent try/catch). Add friendlyModel(). Render a trace-row button (aria-expanded/aria-controls) with count/model/seconds and a conditional trace-panel region listing trace.toolCalls; open state per index in a Set. Add globals.css trace-row/panel/tool rules: OKLCH tokens, mono, 44px, reduced-motion. Run slop-detect.
